Answer: T1059

Scenario:
After gaining a foothold on a Windows host, the adversary opened a TxF transaction on a trusted executable and overwrote it with a malicious image visible only within the transaction. They mapped the tainted image into a section, rolled back the TxF changes to restore the on-disk file, then spawned a process from the in-memory section to run their payload under the guise of the legitimate binary.
response:
The adversary used Windows Transactional NTFS (TxF) to temporarily modify a trusted executable, created a memory-mapped section from the modified (transaction-only) image, then rolled back the transaction so the on-disk file appeared untouched while executing the payload from the in-memory section. This is characteristic of **process hollowing / transacted hollowing**, where a legitimate process image is replaced or executed from a modified in-memory representation to masquerade as a trusted binary.
